what_failed: QD-20260311-C022-ETH-STATE-CHANGE-MOMENTUM-HOLD-v1: valid-data zero-trade outcome via integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples shows the break-and-hold state-change grammar is too sparse.

why_it_failed: Valid zero-trade packets are not execution faults; they are structural density failures that must resolve to refine or abort.
Break-and-hold state-change logic and delayed retest continuation were both too selective for the observed intraday structure.
BTC value reacceptance exhaustion was worse than sparse: when it did trade, it lost decisively on both BTC and ETH, indicating the thesis itself is economically wrong in this environment.
iterate_next: Rotate away from sparse continuation grammar that cannot clear basic participation density on valid data.
Do not rescue BTC value reacceptance exhaustion with cosmetic parameter edits after PF 0.00 valid-data failure.
Prefer denser participation logic and clearer role separation in the next cycle rather than ceremonial hold filters or late retest choreography.
regime_notes: Cycle 22 ran in an ETH 1h chop-dominant intraday regime with TRANSITION 55%, TREND_UP 40%, and CHOP 5%. The tested continuation grammars were too selective, while the exhaustion fade thesis lost when it actually traded.
indicator_role_notes: Combining break, hold, state confirmation, and continuation expectation created event scarcity on ETH/AXS intraday lanes.
Value reacceptance plus exhaustion plus rollover confirmation on BTC produced trades, but the trades were economically wrong rather than merely rare.
management_notes: Management was not the primary issue on the zero-trade strategies; entry grammar never produced participation.
For BTC value reacceptance exhaustion, management changes are not justified before thesis replacement because PF was 0.00 across valid trades.

learning_requirements: Every cycle must yield diagnosis, lesson extraction, and durable learning update.
Zero-trade outcomes require explicit diagnosis and one rescue attempt at most.
PASS should flow into structured refinement variants, not cosmetic tweaks.
Valid zero-trade outcomes must be classified as refine or abort, never fix_only.
